title: Special Playtime
description: A short daily play session where the child leads and the parent follows.

steps.title: Set the Time
steps.detail: Choose a quiet 10–15 minute window with no distractions.

steps.title: Follow the Child
steps.detail: Let your child choose the activity. Give no instructions — just follow their lead.

steps.title: Describe & Imitate
steps.detail: Narrate what your child is doing like a sports announcer and copy their actions.

steps.title: Why It Helps
steps.detail: Builds confidence, independence, and a strong parent-child bond while reducing behavioural issues.

title: Target Practice
description: A fun throwing game that builds coordination and counting.

steps.title: Create Targets
steps.detail: Cut holes in a cardboard box and assign point values to each hole.

steps.title: Take Turns
steps.detail: Throw soft balls or beanbags from increasing distances.

steps.title: Count the Score
steps.detail: Let the child add up points after each round — sneaky maths practice!

steps.title: Why It Helps
steps.detail: Develops hand-eye coordination, gross motor skills, and basic addition.

title: Gratitude Sharing
description: End the day by sharing things you are thankful for.

steps.title: Start with Yourself
steps.detail: Share one thing you felt grateful for today.

steps.title: Ask Your Child
steps.detail: Encourage them to share even small happy moments.

steps.title: Appreciate Together
steps.detail: Acknowledge each other's thoughts and end with positivity.

title: Feelings Check-In
description: A quick daily ritual to name and share emotions together.

steps.title: Pick a Feeling
steps.detail: Ask your child: 'What colour does your mood feel like today?' and share yours too.

steps.title: Name the Feeling
steps.detail: Help them put a word to it — happy, nervous, excited, tired.

steps.title: Why It Helps
steps.detail: Builds emotional vocabulary and makes children feel heard every single day.

title: Gratitude Jar
description: Write or draw one thing you're grateful for and drop it in a jar.

steps.title: Decorate the Jar
steps.detail: Let your child paint or sticker a glass jar to make it special.

steps.title: Write Daily
steps.detail: Each evening write one thing that made you happy on a slip of paper and add it.

steps.title: Read Together
steps.detail: On weekends, read a few slips out loud together.

steps.title: Why It Helps
steps.detail: Builds a gratitude mindset, positive thinking, and family connection.

title: Kitchen Science Lab
description: Fun science experiment that introduces chemistry through play.

steps.title: Prepare Materials
steps.detail: Place a small bottle in a tray and fill it with baking soda and food colouring.

steps.title: Create the Reaction
steps.detail: Let the child slowly pour vinegar and watch the bubbling explosion.

steps.title: Discuss Why
steps.detail: Ask 'why do you think it bubbled?' and guide them to the acid-base idea.

steps.title: Why It Helps
steps.detail: Teaches cause-and-effect and introduces science concepts visually.

title: Mealtime Math
description: Use everyday meals to teach counting and measurement.

steps.title: Count Items
steps.detail: Ask your child to count plates, spoons, or glasses needed for the family.

steps.title: Estimate Volumes
steps.detail: Let the child guess how many spoons of water will fill a cup.

steps.title: Sort & Group
steps.detail: Sort food by colour, shape, or size — a sneaky intro to classification.

steps.title: Why It Helps
steps.detail: Builds early maths skills, estimation, and logical thinking.

title: Floating Ketchup
description: Physics activity demonstrating pressure and buoyancy.

steps.title: Set Up Bottle
steps.detail: Fill a clear plastic bottle with water and drop in a ketchup packet.

steps.title: Squeeze & Release
steps.detail: Squeeze the bottle to make the packet sink and release to make it float.

steps.title: Guess Why
steps.detail: Ask the child to predict what will happen before each squeeze.

steps.title: Why It Helps
steps.detail: Introduces density and pressure concepts through hands-on play.

title: Home Writing Centre
description: Turn writing into play by setting up a fun little post office at home.

steps.title: Set up a fun writing space
steps.detail: Create a small writing corner with paper, envelopes, and pens. Tell your child you're opening a 'post office' and they are in charge. This makes the activity feel exciting instead of like a task.

steps.title: Write something meaningful
steps.detail: Ask them to write or draw a letter for someone — a family member, friend, or even a toy. If they can't write yet, let them draw and tell you what to write. The goal is expression, not perfection.

steps.title: Deliver the message
steps.detail: Deliver letters around the house — under doors or to the kitchen. This makes writing feel real and fun, and children naturally want to do more of it.

title: Story Stones
description: Paint rocks and use them to invent and tell stories together.

steps.title: Paint the Stones
steps.detail: Paint simple pictures on smooth rocks — a sun, a house, a dog, a cloud.

steps.title: Build a Story
steps.detail: Take turns picking a stone and adding a sentence to the story.

steps.title: Why It Helps
steps.detail: Sparks narrative thinking, turn-taking, and imaginative language.

title: Memory Sharing Time
description: Share stories from your childhood with your child.

steps.title: Start with a Story
steps.detail: Tell something from your childhood.

steps.title: Ask Them
steps.detail: Ask if they have similar feelings or stories.

steps.title: Laugh Together
steps.detail: Keep it light and fun.

steps.title: Builds emotional bond
steps.detail: They feel closer to you.

title: Shadow Drawing
description: Use sunlight and objects to trace and create shadow art.

steps.title: Set Up Objects
steps.detail: Place toys or household items on paper in sunlight near a window.

steps.title: Trace the Shadow
steps.detail: Trace the shadow outline and then decorate it however the child likes.

steps.title: Why It Helps
steps.detail: Teaches light and shadow concepts and develops fine motor control.

title: Nature Scavenger Hunt
description: An outdoor adventure that turns a walk into a learning experience.

steps.title: Make a Checklist
steps.detail: Create a list of items — a smooth rock, a Y-shaped stick, a yellow flower.

steps.title: Explore Together
steps.detail: Walk through a park or garden and search for each item together.

steps.title: Sort the Finds
steps.detail: Back home, sort collected items by size, colour, or texture.

steps.title: Why It Helps
steps.detail: Improves observation skills, environmental awareness, and physical activity.

title: LEGO Museum Tour
description: Building play combined with creative storytelling.

steps.title: Build Creations
steps.detail: Let the child build anything they like — no rules on what to make.

steps.title: Museum Tour
steps.detail: Ask the child to explain each creation as if guiding visitors around a museum.

steps.title: Add Labels
steps.detail: Write little title cards for each exhibit like a real museum.

steps.title: Why It Helps
steps.detail: Develops language skills, confidence, spatial reasoning, and storytelling.

title: Build Something Together
description: Create something using whatever you have at home.

steps.title: Pick an Idea
steps.detail: House, car, robot — anything.

steps.title: Work Together
steps.detail: Divide small tasks.

steps.title: Show & Explain
steps.detail: Let your child explain what they made.

steps.title: Encourages teamwork
steps.detail: Builds creativity and confidence.

title: Egg Carton Caterpillar
description: A recycled-material craft that builds fine motor skills.

steps.title: Prepare Carton
steps.detail: Cut an egg carton into a strip — each cup becomes one segment of the caterpillar.

steps.title: Paint Together
steps.detail: Let the child choose colours and paint each segment.

steps.title: Add Details
steps.detail: Poke pipe cleaners through for antennae and draw a face on the front.

steps.title: Why It Helps
steps.detail: Builds fine motor control, creativity, and pride in making something.

title: Flashlight Storytelling
description: A calm bedtime game using imagination and a torch.

steps.title: Dim the Room
steps.detail: Turn off the lights and use a flashlight to illuminate objects one at a time.

steps.title: Create Stories
steps.detail: Take turns telling a short story about whatever the light lands on.

steps.title: Continue the Story
steps.detail: Each person must continue from where the last person left off.

steps.title: Why It Helps
steps.detail: Boosts language development, creativity, and emotional bonding.

title: DIY Bird Feeder
description: Build a simple bird feeder and observe birds together.

steps.title: Coat the Cone
steps.detail: Roll a pine cone in peanut butter until fully covered.

steps.title: Add Seeds
steps.detail: Roll it in birdseed and tie a string around the top to hang it.

steps.title: Observe Together
steps.detail: Hang it outside and keep a notebook to record which birds visit.

steps.title: Why It Helps
steps.detail: Teaches responsibility, patience, and appreciation for nature.

title: Mini Garden Project
description: Plant and grow a small herb or flower garden together.

steps.title: Pick a Plant
steps.detail: Let the child choose a seed — basil, sunflower, and marigold all grow quickly.

steps.title: Plant Together
steps.detail: Fill a pot with soil, make a hole, drop in the seed, and cover it gently.

steps.title: Daily Care
steps.detail: Assign the child as the official plant caretaker — water it every morning.

steps.title: Why It Helps
steps.detail: Teaches responsibility, patience, and the science of plant growth.
